steps.title: Use quadratic regression
steps.content:
The product of two linear factors is a quadratic, so Desmos can fit the expanded form quickly. Enter three $w$-values and regress to $aw^2+bw+c$:
$$w_1=[-1,0,1]$$
$$p_1=(w_1+7)(3w_1-10)$$
$$p_1\sim aw_1^2+bw_1+c$$

steps.title: Read the coefficients
steps.content:
The regression gives $a=3$, $b=11$, and $c=-70$, so the expression is:
$$3w^2+11w-70$$

steps.title: Confirm by multiplying
steps.content:
FOIL gives:
$$(w+7)(3w-10)=3w^2-10w+21w-70$$
Combine the middle terms: $-10w+21w=11w$, so the product is $3w^2+11w-70$.

steps.title: Answer
steps.content: $3w^2+11w-70$ matches choice C, so C is correct.
